948.reason: [The match method does not handle the case when an unexpected token is encountered for other expected types (not SEMICN, RPARENT, or RBRACK). In such cases, it silently fails to report an error or consume a token, which can lead to infinite loops or incorrect parsing. According to the problem description, all syntax errors should be reported with appropriate line numbers and error codes, but this method only handles three specific missing punctuation cases and ignores all other mismatches.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The match method does not handle the case when an unexpected token is encountered for other expected types (not SEMICN, RPARENT, or RBRACK). In such cases, it silently fails to report an error or consume a token, which can lead to infinite loops or incorrect parsing. According to the problem description, all syntax errors should be reported with appropriate line numbers and error codes, but this method only handles three specific missing punctuation cases and ignores all other mismatches.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The method does not handle the case when an unexpected token is encountered for other expected kinds (e.g., ASSIGN, LPARENT, etc.), leading to silent failure or infinite loops instead of reporting an error. Additionally, even for SEMICN/RPARENT/RBRACK, it records the error but does not consume any token or advance parsing, which may cause repeated errors or parser stuck.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The match method does not handle the case when the expected token is missing but it's not one of SEMICN, RPARENT, or RBRACK. In such cases, no error is recorded and parsing continues with incorrect state, potentially causing crashes or wrong output. Additionally, even for the handled cases, the parser does not consume any token or attempt recovery, which may lead to infinite loops or cascading errors.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The method does not handle unexpected tokens for other expectedKind cases, and crucially does not advance the lexer when an error occurs, which can cause infinite loops or repeated error reporting. Additionally, it should consume a token even in error cases to allow parsing to continue.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Missing default case to handle unexpected tokens that don't match expectedKind and aren't one of the special cases above; this leads to silent failures instead of proper error reporting]

941.reason: [Using getTokenByOff(-1) may access an invalid or unrelated token when the current token stream is at the beginning; also, this only handles missing semicolon but ignores other required tokens]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Using getTokenByOff(-1) may access an invalid or unrelated token when the current token stream is at the beginning; also, this only handles missing semicolon but ignores other required tokens]
